description_long: This is a military-style, single-fuselage airplane with a light olive-green exterior and prominent red star insignias on its wings and fuselage, suggesting Soviet or Russian origin. The aircraft features a straight, mid-mounted wing configuration with two visible engines mounted on the wings, each equipped with a propeller. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely accommodating one or two crew members. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it may be retractable or not modeled. The overall design is reminiscent of World War II-era military aircraft, built primarily from metal with some glass components for the cockpit.
description: A military-style olive-green airplane with red star insignias, twin wing-mounted propeller engines, and a conventional tail. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned at the front of the fuselage.
description_view_0: The airplane is seen from below, highlighting the undercarriage, wing structure, and red star insignias on the wings and fuselage.
description_view_1: A rear view showing the full wingspan, twin engines, and the tail's horizontal and vertical stabilizers.
description_view_2: The airplane is viewed from above, displaying the top surfaces of the wings and fuselage, with clear visibility of the cockpit and engine placement.
description_view_3: A direct front view emphasizing the nose, cockpit, propellers, and the symmetry of the wings and engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible dihedral or anhedral angle, no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable or not modeled.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit, likely single or tandem seat with a clear canopy.
